species: Thauera-dominated alkylbenzene-oxidizing nitrate-reducing bacteria (ABO-NRB) enrichment (also Pseudomonas)

growth_records.strain: enrichment from Medicine Hat Glauconitic C field produced water 18PW

up.method: qV=D*Cfeed; effluent nitrate=0 at steady state

sec.method: stoichiometric from nitrate reduction; nitrite transient then 0

growth_records.cite: Suri et al. Microbially Enhanced Oil Recovery by Alkylbenzene-Oxidizing Nitrate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01243. [PMC6591262]
growth_records.snippet: Flow was then started by injection of CSBK medium with 10 mM nitrate and 1.2 mM ethylbenzene ... at a rate of 10 mL/day (dilution rate, D=0.1 d-1)... the effluent concentrations of nitrate and nitrite decreased to zero at day 20 in all cont
growth_records.notes: Continuous (chemostat) culture, ethylbenzene-fed, D=0.1 /d. At steady state mu=D=0.1 /d=0.1/24=0.00417 /h; doubling_time=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.00417=166.3 h. Volumetric uptake qV=D*Cfeed: nitrate=0.1/d*10 mM=1.0 mmol/L/d; ethylbenzene=0.1/d*1.2 mM=0.12 mmol/L/d (both fully consumed, effluent=0). Biomass at 65 d: OD600=3.12 (no gDW reported) so per-gDW rates not derivable. Community (Thauera 17.8% at d23 rising to 52.9% at d63; Pseudomonas 16.6%->7.8%), not a pure strain. N2 secretion is stoichiometric inference (2 NO3->N2), not measured.

growth_records.strain: enrichment from Medicine Hat Glauconitic C field produced water 18PW
growth_records.cite: Suri et al. Microbially Enhanced Oil Recovery by Alkylbenzene-Oxidizing Nitrate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01243. [PMC6591262]
growth_records.snippet: Increasing the rate of medium influx to 20 mL/day (D=0.2 d-1) gave incomplete reduction of nitrate and nitrite with effluent concentrations of up to 5 to 7 mM from day 35 to 65 after which the effluent nitrate and nitrite concentrations rem
growth_records.notes: Ethylbenzene-fed chemostat at D=0.2 /d. mu=D=0.2/d=0.00833 /h; doubling_time=0.6931/0.00833=83.2 h. Volumetric qV=D*Cfeed: nitrate=2.0, ethylbenzene=0.24 mmol/L/d. Full consumption only after re-equilibration (effluent returned to 0 after day 65). No gDW biomass -> no per-gDW rates. Community, not pure strain.

growth_records.strain: enrichment from Medicine Hat Glauconitic C field produced water 18PW
growth_records.cite: Suri et al. Microbially Enhanced Oil Recovery by Alkylbenzene-Oxidizing Nitrate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01243. [PMC6591262]
growth_records.snippet: During flow the continuous cultures received medium with 10 mM nitrate and 1.4 mM of aqueous toluene... effluent concentrations of nitrate and nitrite decreased to zero at day 20; biomass at 65 days OD600=3.35 (toluene-fed)
growth_records.notes: Toluene-fed chemostat, D=0.1 /d. mu=D=0.00417 /h; doubling_time=166.3 h. qV: nitrate=1.0, toluene=0.14 mmol/L/d. Biomass OD600=3.35 at 65 d (toluene) vs 3.12 (ethylbenzene); no gDW. Community, not pure strain.

growth_records.strain: enrichment from Medicine Hat Glauconitic C field produced water 18PW
growth_records.cite: Suri et al. Microbially Enhanced Oil Recovery by Alkylbenzene-Oxidizing Nitrate-Reducing Bacteria. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.01243. [PMC6591262]
growth_records.snippet: Increasing the rate of medium influx to 20 mL/day (D=0.2 d-1)... after which the effluent nitrate and nitrite concentrations remained at zero
growth_records.notes: Toluene-fed chemostat at D=0.2 /d. mu=D=0.00833 /h; doubling_time=83.2 h. qV: nitrate=2.0, toluene=0.28 mmol/L/d. Community, not pure strain; no gDW biomass.
